# M8-SDL3 — Hardware Test Spec (`m8_makeprobe` + `m8_capture`)

How to validate the probe generator and the capture rig against a **real M8 headless**,
with the pass/fail decided by machine, not by a human ear. Written after
`M8_CAPTURE_SPEC.md` — the tools it specified now exist, and three more tools built since
(`m8_spectrum`, `m8_analyze --events/--json`, `m8_render --load --note`) let us close the
loop objectively.

**Goal:** prove that a probe generated by `m8_makeprobe` loads and plays on hardware, that
`m8_capture` records it cleanly, and that the recording is the *right note* — with as few
human touches as the hardware physically allows, and a single machine-readable verdict at
the end.

**Prerequisite reading:** `M8_CAPTURE_SPEC.md` (what the two tools do), `AGENTS.md` (build
rules, honesty rules, hardware-constant rules), `status.md` (current state).

---

## 0. Ground truth — what the tools actually do today

Do not spec against the *intent* of `M8_CAPTURE_SPEC.md`; spec against the code. Verified by
reading `src/tools/main_makeprobe.cpp` and `src/tools/main_capture.cpp`:

| Fact | Consequence for the test |
|---|---|
| `m8_makeprobe` only **round-trips `--type macrosynth`** (`verifyRoundTrip` hard-checks `holds_alternative<MacroSynth>`; wav/fm/hyper write then fail verify → exit 1). | The only probe that survives generation today is MacroSynth. |
| There is **no sampler probe type**. | We cannot make a parity-matched probe (see §2). Timbre self-test is blocked until a sampler probe exists (§9). |
| Our engine renders **MacroSynth as a polyBLEP saw**, hardware renders it as **Braids**. | `m8_spectrum` timbre distance between capture and render will be **large and that is expected** — it is the synth gap, not a tool bug. Only **pitch** and **WAV health** are synth-independent. |
| `m8_capture --batch` is **parsed but not implemented** (no loop over the batch file; single capture only). | Batch (Tier 2) requires finishing `--batch` first (§9). |
| `m8_capture` START mask (`0x08`) and STOP (`KEY_A 0x10`) are **guesses** — the source says so, and the "button table in status.md" it cites **does not exist in the repo**. | Step 1 of every hardware run is to **empirically pin the button protocol** (§5). Nothing downstream is trustworthy until it is. |
| `m8_capture` has **no file-load-over-serial** capability. | The probe must be on the SD card and loaded on the device before capture. Removing that last human step is Tier 3 (§8), and is real new work. |
| `m8_capture` trims to onset (threshold 0.01, 5 ms pre-roll) and writes 48 kHz/16-bit stereo. | The captured WAV's onset is ≈ sample 0 by construction; health checks must account for that. |
| `m8_render --load p.m8s` writes `<out>.wav` **and** `<out>_events.csv`. | The render is our **pitch oracle** (§2) and gives the exact expected note/timing with zero hardware. |

---

## 1. The one honest constraint

The M8 headless speaks two things over its single USB cable that we use: a **serial control
channel** (button presses) and a **USB audio input** (its output). It does **not** expose a
"load this file" command — loading a song means driving the on-device file browser. So there
is exactly one action that is genuinely hard to automate: **getting a specific probe loaded
on the device.**

Everything else — generate, play, record, trim, analyse, compare, verdict — is fully
automatable *today*. This spec therefore automates all of that now (Tier 1), and specs the
removal of the last human step as an explicit, optional, clearly-scoped tier (Tier 3) rather
than pretending it is free.

---

## 2. The core idea — the render is the oracle, pitch is synth-independent

We have no golden reference for what hardware *should* sound like — that is the whole reason
the parity loop exists. But we do not need one to test `makeprobe` and `capture`, because two
properties are true regardless of how good our synth is:

1. **Pitch.** A note at MIDI 60 has the same fundamental whether hardware plays it as Braids
   or we render it as a saw. So `m8_render --load probe.m8s` gives us the **exact expected
   fundamental** with no hardware at all, and `m8_spectrum --ref capture.wav --test
   render.wav` reports whether the capture's fundamental matches. A pitch match proves:
   `makeprobe` encoded the note correctly **and** hardware loaded/played that note **and**
   `capture` recorded it at the right rate/pitch. One check, whole chain.

2. **Health.** A clean single-note capture is a sustained tone: no clipping, no NaN, onset
   near sample 0, ≥ 1.5 s of sustain, non-trivial RMS. `m8_analyze --json` measures all of
   this. It proves `capture` recorded, played, and trimmed correctly.

**Timbre is deliberately *not* asserted equal** with the current toolset, because our
MacroSynth is a saw. `m8_spectrum` still *reports* the log-spectral distance and the harmonic
table — record them, but the acceptance gate is pitch + health, not distance. §9 explains how
a sampler probe would make timbre an objective gate too.

> **Shape choice matters.** Use a MacroSynth shape that produces a **clearly pitched** tone
> (e.g. `--shape 0x00`). Some Braids models are noise/percussion with no defined fundamental;
> those cannot anchor the pitch check. Pin the shape in the fixture (§4) and do not sweep it
> for the *self-test* — sweeping is for the parity library later.

---

## 3. Automation tiers

Each tier is a superset of the one below. Pick the highest tier the hardware and your
appetite for new code support. Tier 1 is the recommended baseline and is achievable with
zero new code beyond the orchestrator glue.

| Tier | Human touches | New code needed | What it proves |
|---|---|---|---|
| **T0** | none (no hardware) | none | `makeprobe` writes a valid, self-consistent `.m8s`. |
| **T1** | **one**: load one probe on the device, then walk away | orchestrator glue only | Full chain for one probe: load→play→record→trim→analyse→compare→verdict, machine-decided. |
| **T2** | **one per probe**: load each probe, press a key | finish `m8_capture --batch` | Same, across a whole reference library, semi-automated. |
| **T3** | **zero** | serial file-load (framebuffer decode) + optional SD mass-storage copy | Fully unattended: harness loads each probe itself. |

---

## 4. The fixture

One canonical self-test probe, generated fresh each run so the test also exercises
`makeprobe`:

```
m8_makeprobe --type macrosynth --shape 0x00 --timbre 0x40 --color 0x80 \
             --note C-4 --tempo 120 --out probe_selftest.m8s
```

- `--type macrosynth` — the only type that round-trips today (§0).
- `--shape 0x00` — a pitched Braids model (§2 note).
- `--note C-4` — MIDI 60, our sampler root, unambiguous. Expected fundamental ≈ **261.63 Hz**.
- The built-in amp envelope (AHD→VOLUME, hold 0x80 / decay 0x80 at 120 BPM) sustains
  ~5 s — ample for a ≥ 1.5 s analysis window.

`makeprobe` exits non-zero if its own software round-trip fails, so a zero exit is the first
(T0) gate before any hardware is touched.

---

## 5. Step 1 (foundational) — pin the button protocol

**Nothing downstream is valid until this passes.** The START/STOP masks in `m8_capture` are
unverified guesses (§0). Confirm them empirically, using the captured audio itself as the
oracle — no human ear, no screen decode:

**Procedure (with the self-test probe already loaded on the device):**

1. Start audio capture, send the candidate START mask, record 2 s, stop.
2. `m8_analyze capture.wav --json`. **Playback started iff** the WAV is non-silent:
   `peak > 0.02` and `longest_silence_sec < 0.5` (a loaded, playing probe is a sustained
   tone; a wrong mask leaves the device idle → near silence).
3. If silent, the mask is wrong. Sweep candidate masks (`0x01`, `0x02`, `0x04`, `0x08`,
   `0x10`, …) until one produces sound. Record the winner.
4. Confirm STOP the same way: after a working START, send the candidate STOP mask, then
   capture 1 s and assert `peak < 0.02` (device stopped).

**Output:** write the confirmed masks to `hw_buttons.json`
(`{"start_mask": N, "stop_mask": M, "confirmed": true}`). This file is a **recorded
hardware constant** from then on (treat it like the constants in `AGENTS.md` §7 — do not
re-guess it once pinned). If `m8_capture`'s hard-coded masks disagree with the confirmed
ones, **fix `m8_capture`** and say so; do not paper over it in the harness.

> This step doubles as the cheapest possible proof that the whole rig is alive: serial
> reaches the device, audio reaches the host, and the probe is loaded and playable.

---

## 6. Step 2 — the automated chain (Tier 1)

With buttons pinned and the probe loaded, run this sequence. Every step is a real flag on a
real tool; no human judgement anywhere.

```
# 1. (T0) generate + software round-trip — exits non-zero on failure
m8_makeprobe --type macrosynth --shape 0x00 --timbre 0x40 --color 0x80 \
             --note C-4 --tempo 120 --out probe_selftest.m8s

# 2. render the SAME probe locally — this is the pitch oracle (writes .wav + _events.csv)
m8_render --load probe_selftest.m8s --note C-4 --instrument 0 --seconds 3 --out render_selftest

# 3. capture the probe off hardware (probe already loaded on device)
m8_capture --port COM4 --audio M8 --seconds 3 --pre-roll 5 --out capture_selftest.wav

# 4. health of the capture (machine-readable; see §6.1 for which fields gate)
m8_analyze capture_selftest.wav --json capture_health.json

# 5. pitch: capture vs render oracle (fundamental match is the gate; distance is recorded only)
m8_spectrum --ref capture_selftest.wav --test render_selftest.wav --json pitch_compare.json
```

### 6.1 Acceptance — read specific JSON fields, not the tools' overall pass/fail

**Do not gate on `m8_analyze`'s `hard_checks.overall_pass`.** Those checks are calibrated for
full-mix music renders; a clean sustained single tone legitimately has **crest ≈ 3 dB** (a
pure sine's crest factor is 3.01 dB) and would false-fail the `crest > 6 dB` check. Gate on
the synth-independent fields instead:

**Capture health** (`capture_health.json`):
- `metrics.non_finite == 0`
- `metrics.peak < 1.0` and `metrics.peak > 0.05` (recorded something, not clipping)
- `metrics.longest_silence_sec < 0.5` (no dropout mid-note)
- `duration_sec >= 1.5` (enough sustain to analyse)

**Pitch** (`pitch_compare.json`):
- `fundamental_ok == true` (the tool already flags a match within ~2 % / ~34 cents)
- and, belt-and-braces, `abs(fundamental_ref_hz - 261.63) / 261.63 < 0.03` (the *captured*
  fundamental is actually C-4, not merely equal to whatever the render produced)

**Recorded, not gated** (for the log / trend, and to feed Macrosyn work later):
- `pitch_compare.json`: `log_spectral_distance_db`, the harmonic table, both centroids.
  Expected to be **large** (saw vs Braids) — a small distance here would actually be
  surprising and worth a look, but it is not a pass/fail.

A run **passes** iff `makeprobe` exited 0 **and** every capture-health field **and** both
pitch conditions hold.

---

## 7. The orchestrator — `m8_hwtest` (one command, one verdict)

Wrap §5–§6 in a single driver so a run is one invocation and one JSON verdict. It is glue —
chain the existing exes, parse their `--json` outputs, emit a verdict. Implement as a
PowerShell script (`tools/m8_hwtest.ps1`, no build target — it is a test harness, not
product) or a small C++ `m8_hwtest` if you prefer a target; the **logic and the verdict
schema below are the spec**, the language is not.

```
m8_hwtest --port COM4 --audio M8 --out-dir hwtest_out/
          [--skip-button-pin]        # reuse an existing hw_buttons.json
          [--tier 1|2|3]             # default 1
```

**Behaviour:**
1. Generate the fixture (§4). Abort with verdict `makeprobe_failed` on non-zero exit.
2. Render the oracle (§6 step 2).
3. If no confirmed `hw_buttons.json` (and not `--skip-button-pin`), run §5. Abort with
   `button_protocol_unconfirmed` if no mask produces sound.
4. Capture (§6 step 3), analyse, compare (§6 steps 4–5).
5. Evaluate §6.1 and write `hwtest_out/verdict.json`:

```json
{
  "pass": true,
  "tier": 1,
  "makeprobe_roundtrip": "pass",
  "button_protocol": { "start_mask": 8, "stop_mask": 16, "confirmed": true },
  "capture_health": {
    "non_finite": 0, "peak": 0.71, "longest_silence_sec": 0.00,
    "duration_sec": 2.83, "pass": true
  },
  "pitch": {
    "fundamental_ref_hz": 261.4, "fundamental_test_hz": 261.6,
    "fundamental_ok": true, "within_c4": true, "pass": true
  },
  "timbre_recorded_not_gated": {
    "log_spectral_distance_db": 12.7, "centroid_ref_hz": 1980, "centroid_test_hz": 3120
  },
  "failure": null
}
```

**Exit codes:** `0` pass · `1` a gate failed (`failure` names which) · `2` rig/setup error
(port not found, audio device not found, probe not loaded → detected as silence at step 5).

The harness prints the command and the parsed numbers, never a summary in place of them
(`AGENTS.md` §4). It runs no per-sample loops (`AGENTS.md` §2) — it consumes the tools'
already-computed JSON.

---

## 8. Tier 3 — removing the last human step (unattended)

Optional, and the only part that is real new engineering. Two independent pieces; do the
copy first (cheaper, lower risk) and treat load-nav as the stretch.

### 8.1 Automating the file copy (if the SD mounts as a drive)

**Operator-confirmed prerequisite:** does *your* headless present its SD card as USB mass
storage while the serial + audio interfaces are active? This is device/firmware-dependent —
**do not assume it; confirm it once and record the answer** in `hwtest_out/rig.json`. If yes,
the harness copies `probes/*.m8s` to the SD drive letter directly (`Copy-Item`), eliminating
the manual card shuffle. If no, the operator copies the library once per session (still one
touch for the whole batch, not per-probe).

### 8.2 Automating the load (serial browser navigation)

The device must be *told* to load a file. Two sub-options:

- **8.2a Blind keystrokes** — from a known boot/home state, send a fixed arrow-key sequence
  to reach and select the probe. Simple, **fragile**: any state drift loads the wrong file,
  and there is no way to know. Acceptable only if the harness re-homes deterministically and
  the sequence is verified per firmware. Use the pinned button masks (§5) for the direction
  keys — which means §5 must first confirm the *direction* masks too, not just START/STOP.

- **8.2b Framebuffer-verified navigation (robust)** — decode the M8 serial display protocol
  (SLIP-framed draw commands, same protocol `m8c` speaks) into a text grid — conceptually the
  same shadow-grid idea as our own `Renderer` (`M8_UI_HARNESS_SPEC.md`). Then navigate the
  file browser reading the screen to confirm the highlighted filename before selecting, and
  to confirm "now playing" after START. This is the only genuinely reliable unattended path.

  Spec it as a new capability — extend `m8_capture` with `--load-file <name>` (or a small
  `m8_nav` helper) that: opens serial, enables display, decodes the framebuffer, drives the
  browser to `<name>`, confirms the filename on screen, loads it, and returns success/fail.

  **Acceptance (8.2b):** given a probe on the SD card, `--load-file probe_selftest.m8s`
  leaves the device with that song loaded (confirmed by a subsequent §5/§6 capture producing
  the right pitch), across 10 consecutive runs with no human touch, zero wrong-file loads.

> Scope honestly: 8.2b is a protocol decoder plus a state machine. It is worth building *once*
> because it converts the entire parity library from "one human load per probe" to fully
> unattended overnight runs — but it is not required for Tier 1/2 value, and `AGENTS.md` §5
> says do not build it until we commit to it. This spec defines it; it does not schedule it.

---

## 9. Enabling improvements (tool changes that raise the ceiling)

Not part of the test itself — but each removes a real limitation found in §0. Marked so they
are not mistaken for finished work.

1. **Sampler probe in `m8_makeprobe`** (`--type sampler --sample <wav>`). The sampler is at
   hardware parity, so a sampler probe makes **timbre an objective gate**: hardware-capture vs
   our-render of the *same* sampler probe should give a **small** `log_spectral_distance_db`.
   That turns §6.1's "recorded, not gated" line into a hard check and gives the strongest
   possible end-to-end proof of the whole chain. Requires: sampler instrument construction in
   `makeprobe`, a bundled known WAV (e.g. a pure sine) copied to the SD card alongside the
   probe, and extending `verifyRoundTrip` to the sampler variant.
2. **Fix `verifyRoundTrip` for non-macrosynth** — today it fails wav/fm/hyper probes even
   though they write fine (§0). Either make it type-aware or it will keep exiting 1 on those
   types.
3. **Implement `m8_capture --batch`** — currently parsed and ignored (§0). Needed for Tier 2:
   loop the `name<TAB>label` list, prompt-and-capture per entry.
4. **Reconcile the button masks** — once §5 pins them, hard-code the confirmed values in
   `m8_capture` and add the button table to `status.md`'s hardware-constants section (it is
   referenced there but missing). Kills the recurring "the masks are a guess" footgun.
5. **A `--json` verdict already exists** for `m8_analyze` and `m8_spectrum` (built this cycle)
   — the orchestrator consumes them directly; no change needed there.

---

## 10. Order of work

1. **T0 in CI** — a test that runs `m8_makeprobe` and asserts exit 0 + the file re-loads
   through `m8-files-cxx`. No hardware; pure regression value now.
2. **Pin the button protocol (§5)** on the real device. One evening. Produces
   `hw_buttons.json` and, per §9.4, a fix to `m8_capture` + a `status.md` entry.
3. **`m8_hwtest` Tier 1 (§7)** — the orchestrator. One command, one verdict, one human load.
   This is the deliverable that makes hardware validation repeatable.
4. **§9.1 sampler probe** — the biggest objectivity win; turns timbre into a gate.
5. **Tier 2** — finish `--batch` (§9.3), run the whole sweep library semi-automated.
6. **Tier 3** — only if unattended overnight runs are worth the decoder (§8.2b).

Steps 1–3 need only the current tools plus glue. 4–6 are the enabling improvements, each
independently useful.

---

## 11. Acceptance summary (what "the test passes" means)

- **`m8_makeprobe`:** exit 0 on the fixture; the file re-loads through `m8-files-cxx` with the
  instrument/note/chain/song fields intact (T0, `verifyRoundTrip` already does this in-tool).
- **`m8_capture`:** on a pre-loaded probe, produces a WAV with `non_finite == 0`,
  `0.05 < peak < 1.0`, `longest_silence_sec < 0.5`, `duration_sec >= 1.5`, onset trimmed to
  ≈ sample 0 (by construction).
- **The chain (pitch):** `m8_spectrum` reports `fundamental_ok == true` and the captured
  fundamental is within 3 % of C-4 (261.63 Hz).
- **Verdict:** `m8_hwtest` exits 0 and writes `verdict.json` with `pass: true`.

Timbre parity is explicitly **out of scope** for this test until a sampler probe exists (§9.1)
— with the current MacroSynth-only, saw-not-Braids toolset, a large spectral distance between
capture and render is the expected, correct result, not a failure.
